title: Service Discovery Record (v1)
description: A single mDNS/DNS-SD service instance advertisement as observed on one network interface, published (retained) to {base}/v1/{service_type}/{interface}/{percent_encoded_instance}. Address classification (scope, APIPA, link-local, ...) is a derived, client-side concern computed from the address value and is deliberately NOT on the wire.

schema_version.description: Contract major version. Redundant with the /v1/ topic segment, by design.

instance_name.description: DNS-SD service instance label (unencoded UTF-8). The topic segment carries a percent-encoded form of this string.

interface.description: Network interface the advertisement was observed on, e.g. eth0. The addresses are the candidates reachable via THIS interface (bind with SO_BINDTODEVICE).

port.description: SRV port as advertised. A client may deliberately use a different port than advertised.

addresses.description: The CURRENT set of addresses advertised for this instance on this interface. Reflects present reality only; addresses are never carried forward. May legitimately be empty transiently, or contain only IPv6 (or only link-local) entries; consumers must not drop IPv6-only records.

txt.description: DNS-SD TXT record key/values (RFC 6763). Keys and values are strings.

state.description: active = live advertisement; removed = tombstone (goodbye / TTL expiry / browse ItemRemove observed). A removed record carries the full last-known fields plus removed_at.

first_seen.description: When (service_type, interface, instance) was first observed. ISO 8601 UTC.

last_seen.description: When the advertisement was most recently confirmed. Authoritative freshness; age = now - last_seen. ISO 8601 UTC.

ttl_seconds.description: Advertised mDNS record TTL (or the publisher's expected refresh window). Lets a client judge staleness before a tombstone arrives.

removed_at.description: When the record was tombstoned. Present if and only if state == removed. ISO 8601 UTC.

address.description: Literal IP address. Link-local IPv6 (fe80::/10) is published without a zone id; a client binds via the record's interface. Scope/APIPA/link-local classification is derived client-side from this value.

family.description: Address family. Derivable from the literal, kept explicit for readability.
